title: Representations of Kauffman bracket skein algebras of a surface
speaker: Helen Wong
affiliation: Carleton University; von Neumann Fellow, School of Mathematics
abstract: The definition of the Kauffman bracket skein algebra of an oriented surface was originally motivated by the Jones polynomial invariant of knots and links in space, and a representation of the skein algebra features in Witten's topological quantum field theory interpretation of the Jones invariant. Later, the skein algebra and its representations was discovered to bear deep relationships to hyperbolic geometry, via the $SL_2 \mathbb C$-character variety of the surface.

proof_stages.label: From quantum topology to the surface skein algebra
proof_stages.summary: The talk motivates skein algebras from the Jones polynomial and Witten invariants, defines the Kauffman bracket by crossing and disk-loop relations, and forms the Kauffman bracket skein algebra of a thickened surface using stacking, which is generally noncommutative.

proof_stages.label: TQFT and gluing motivation for representations
proof_stages.summary: Heegaard splittings and topological quantum field theory make surface skein algebras act on skein modules/vector spaces, so understanding their irreducible representations is presented as a route to computing and interpreting quantum 3-manifold invariants.

proof_stages.label: Baseline algebraic structure of skein algebras
proof_stages.summary: The speaker reviews commutative exceptions, central puncture loops, basis and generation results, and recent structural theorems such as no zero divisors, Noetherianity, near-Azumaya behavior, and descriptions of the center.

proof_stages.label: Classical character variety at q=1
proof_stages.summary: Hyperbolic metrics and Teichmüller space lead to the SL2(C) character variety, whose trace functions satisfy the skein relation, giving an isomorphism between the q=1 skein algebra and the coordinate ring of the character variety.

proof_stages.label: Deformation quantization viewpoint
proof_stages.summary: For q away from 1, the skein relation is viewed as a noncommutative deformation of the q=1 algebra, with the first-order commutator recovering Goldman's Poisson bracket and representations playing the role of points in the quantization.

proof_stages.label: Quantum Teichmüller embedding
proof_stages.summary: A second quantization using ideal triangulations and q-commuting shear variables produces a quantum Teichmüller algebra and an explicit injective homomorphism from the skein algebra into an algebra whose multiplication and representations are much easier to control.

proof_stages.label: Root-of-unity representation classification theorem
proof_stages.summary: At an odd root of unity, irreducible representations are stated to be controlled by puncture invariants and a classical shadow in the SL2(C) character variety, with existence for prescribed data and generic one-to-one uniqueness.

proof_stages.label: Chebyshev centrality and construction proof
proof_stages.summary: Chebyshev threading at roots of unity produces central skeins satisfying the classical skein relation, so Schur’s lemma extracts the classical shadow, while the quantum Teichmüller embedding constructs representations and puncture-free surfaces are handled by drilling punctures and taking suitable invariant eigenspaces.
